steps.title: Identify where elaboration is introduced
steps.content: The phrase “is well known for” requires an object—what is it known for? The sentence elaborates: “its geographical position in the center of the island.” This elaboration must be clearly separated from the cause it explains.

steps.title: Apply the colon rule
steps.content:
A colon introduces an elaboration or explanation. “Known for:” correctly uses a colon to introduce what the area is known for. Option A leaves the elaboration unpunctuated, creating ambiguous phrasing; option C incorrectly separates the phrasal verb “known for”; option D misuses a semicolon that would suggest independent clauses follow.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
